item.title: Vowrite 0.2.2.0
item.description:
What's New in 0.2.2.0

Added

  • F-082 Model catalog refresh (2026-07) + three new providers: Model pickers now offer each provider's newest generation — Claude Sonnet 5 (new Claude default), GPT-5.6 (luna/sol), Gemini 3.5 Flash + 3.1 Flash-Lite, Qwen 3.7, GLM-5.2, MiniMax M3, Doubao Seed 2.1 — with reasoning/thinking pre-disabled wherever the provider allows it, so polish stays fast. Three new polish providers join: xAI (Grok 4.3/4.5), Cerebras (free tier, ~3000 tok/s — the fastest polish backend available), and Baidu Qianfan (ERNIE 4.5 Turbo). OpenRouter can now also be used for speech-to-text (hosted Whisper large-v3 at aggregator pricing, plus Qwen3-ASR with strong Chinese support). Retired upstream IDs were removed — most notably DeepSeek's legacy `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-reasoner` aliases, which DeepSeek shuts down on 2026-07-24: if one is still selected in your settings, re-pick a DeepSeek V4 model.
  • F-077 Command template library: Creating a Mode no longer starts from a blank prompt — a new "From Template…" option in Personalization offers 15 built-in commands (Improve Writing, Fix Grammar, Make Professional/Casual/Confident/Friendly, Make Longer/Shorter, Simplify, Paraphrase, TL;DR, Summarize in 3 Bullets, Find Action Items, Pros & Cons, Explain Like I'm 5). Each operates on your selected text, with what you speak as extra guidance; the picked template pre-fills the normal Mode editor, so the result is a regular Mode you can rename, edit, and bind to ⌃1–9.
  • F-081 Per-app auto Mode: Settings → Personalization → Per-App Modes lets you map apps to Modes (e.g. Mail → Email, VS Code → Code) — when you start dictating, the mapped Mode is applied for that session automatically, shown as a badge on the recording overlay. Off by default, and your manual choices always win: explicitly switching Modes pins your pick, translation hotkey sessions are never affected, and the global current Mode is never silently changed. Add mappings by picking from running apps or entering a bundle ID.
  • F-079 Language region variants: Language pickers (default language, translation source/target, per-Mode) now offer a second-level region choice — 中文 简体/繁體（台灣）/繁體（香港）, English US/UK/AU, Español ES/MX, Português BR/PT, Français FR/CA. Picking 繁體 biases speech recognition toward Traditional characters and instructs the AI polish/translation to use that region's conventions (e.g. British spelling, Taiwan Traditional Chinese). Existing settings are untouched; leaving the region as default behaves exactly as before.
  • F-080 Personalization is now visible and controllable: Settings → Personalization gains a Learning section showing what Vowrite has learned from your corrections — learned-rule and vocabulary counts plus the three most recent learned corrections (e.g. "语音识别 → Vowrite"). A master toggle turns auto-learning off entirely (replacing the old General → "Auto-learn corrections" toggle — same setting, new home), and "Clear Learned Data" deletes everything learned (your manually created rules are never touched). Learned corrections are now stored as full replacement rules, so they're applied to future dictations, not just used as recognition hints.
  • F-078 Markdown output style: A new built-in "Markdown" output style produces GitHub-flavored Markdown — spoken structure cues become real syntax (named sections → headings, enumerations → lists, emphasis → bold, commands/filenames → inline code, spoken code → fenced blocks with a language tag). Short unstructured dictation stays a plain paragraph; headings are never invented. Pick it like any other style, per Mode or globally.
  • F-074 Vocabulary CSV import/export: Personal vocabulary now has Import / Export buttons in the Vocabulary settings page. Export produces a single-column CSV (one word per line, sorted alphabetically, UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility); import accepts the same format and skips blank lines and `#`-prefixed comments. Round-trips through Excel without corruption. Imports dedupe automatically against your existing list and surface a brief `Imported N, skipped M duplicates` status.

Changed

  • F-072 Polish output style is more assertive (Typeless-inspired): The default polish prompt now treats your dictation as a rough draft to be edited, not a transcript to preserve. Output is more concise and structured — short paragraphs by default, bullets when you enumerate two or more items, the main point surfaced first when you buried it. Language preservation across mixed-language speech (e.g. 中英混合) is unchanged. If you prefer a near-passthrough record of what you said, switch to `Dictation` mode (Cmd+1).

Fixed

  • Stored DeepSeek legacy aliases migrate automatically before the upstream shutdown: DeepSeek retires `deepseek-chat` / `deepseek-reasoner` on 2026-07-24. A one-time migration rewrites any stored alias to `deepseek-v4-flash` — in the split polish config, per-mode model overrides, and saved API presets — and the fresh-install default was updated, so dictation keeps working through the sunset without touching settings.
  • Polish no longer waits on hidden "thinking" with several providers: DeepSeek V4 Flash (the DeepSeek default) was running with chain-of-thought enabled — DeepSeek's documented default — adding seconds of latency to every polish; it is now explicitly disabled. MiniMax M2.x models turn out to ignore the thinking-off parameter upstream (officially documented), so those entries are now labeled honestly and MiniMax M3 — the only MiniMax model that can skip thinking — is the new MiniMax default. Zhipu GLM-4.6 and Doubao Seed entries also gained explicit thinking-off parameters, and Claude Sonnet 5 / Opus 4.8 requests omit `temperature` (those models reject non-default values outright).
  • A failed AI polish or translation is now clearly announced instead of silently pasting the raw transcript: If the polish/translate provider fails (expired key, no credit, network error), Vowrite still outputs the raw transcription as a fallback — but previously it played the *success* sound and gave no indication anything went wrong (only a translation failure left a note buried in the menu-bar dropdown). Now any polish or translation failure plays the error sound and shows a visible toast ("润色失败，已输出原文" / "翻译失败，已输出原文"), so you always know when what you got was the unpolished transcript.
  • Text injection no longer destroys your clipboard's images, files, or rich text: Pasting the transcribed/polished text at your cursor used to save only the clipboard's plain-text string before overwriting it — anything else you had copied (an image, a file, rich formatting) was silently lost forever once the dictation finished pasting. The full clipboard — every item, with all of its original data types — is now snapshotted and restored after the paste completes. The restore is also now guarded against a race: if you copy something new while the paste is still in flight, Vowrite detects that and leaves your new clipboard content alone instead of clobbering it a couple seconds later.
  • A failed paste now shows an error instead of silently doing nothing: If Vowrite couldn't confirm your previous app regained focus, it would abort the paste to avoid typing into the wrong window — correctly — but gave no indication anything had gone wrong, so a dictation could just vanish with no output and no error. This now surfaces a clear "粘贴失败，请手动复制文本" error, and the transcribed text stays available in the app (Last Result / History) for a manual copy.
  • A failed audio write no longer produces a silent, unexplained empty recording: If the audio engine failed to write a buffer to the temporary recording file (e.g. disk full), that failure was previously swallowed entirely — you'd get a truncated/empty recording with no error message. Write failures are now tracked and surfaced as a distinct "录音写入失败，请重试" error when you stop recording, instead of silently sending a broken file to transcription.
  • OAuth sign-in no longer silently drops on a transient server hiccup: A background token refresh (Kimi Code or OpenAI Codex OAuth) used to delete your stored credentials on ANY failure to parse the response — including a temporary 500 error or an HTML error page from a proxy — forcing a full re-sign-in for something that would have worked on retry. The token is now only deleted when the server explicitly reports the refresh token itself as invalid/expired/revoked; anything else is treated as transient and retried next cycle.
  • Recording no longer stalls up to a minute waiting on a hung OAuth token refresh: The 3-second timeout that's supposed to bound the pre-recording credential refresh wasn't actually enforced — a slow/hung refresh request could stall the start of recording for up to 60 seconds (the network default). The wait is now genuinely capped at 3 seconds; the refresh keeps running in the background afterward so the token is still up to date for next time. OAuth refresh requests also now carry a 15-second network timeout instead of the unbounded default.
  • Kimi Code sign-out no longer leaves API-key requests routed to the coding-plan endpoint: Saving settings while signed into Kimi Code (OAuth) could persist the session-scoped coding endpoint (`api.kimi.com/coding/v1`) as your regular STT/Polish base URL. After signing out, plain API-key requests kept hitting that endpoint and were rejected with a 403. Settings writes now always store the plain configured URL; a one-time repair also resets any base URL already corrupted by this bug back to the provider's default.
  • Reasoning-model thinking no longer leaks into polished text: When polishing with a reasoning model (e.g. DeepSeek-R1), the model's internal chain-of-thought could occasionally appear in your output. Two cases the previous filter missed are now stripped: output that begins with reasoning followed only by a closing `` tag — a known DeepSeek-R1 chat-template behaviour where the opening tag is dropped — and tags emitted in uppercase. Polished output stays clean across providers — including the live streaming preview, which previously flashed the raw `` reasoning token-by-token before the final text was cleaned.
  • No more crash on a malformed custom endpoint URL: If a custom base URL was malformed — for example a pasted address with a stray space inside the host — the app could crash while building the request. It now surfaces a clear "Invalid … URL" error instead, so a typo in settings never takes the app down. Covers the polish path and the OpenAI-compatible and Qwen (DashScope) speech-to-text paths. A trailing slash on a custom base URL (e.g. `…/v1/`) is also normalized away, so request endpoints no longer end up with a double slash that some servers reject.
  • Punctuation-only noise no longer dictated: On silence or background noise, speech-to-text sometimes emits a punctuation- or symbol-only fragment (e.g. `!!!` or `。。`). The hallucination filter now drops these, alongside the existing "Thank you for watching" / `[BLANK_AUDIO]` filtering. Real speech in any language (including digits and CJK) is untouched.
  • Recording temp files always cleaned up: A failed transcription (network error, oversized file, bad endpoint) no longer leaves the temporary audio recording behind in the system temp directory — cleanup now runs on every path, not just on success.
  • Excel/Windows vocabulary CSV import now works: Importing a vocabulary CSV saved by Excel (or any Windows editor that uses CRLF line endings) previously collapsed every line into one giant entry, because the CRLF pair wasn't recognized as a line break. Line endings are now normalized, so CRLF, LF, and old-Mac CR files all import correctly — matching the Excel-friendly BOM the export already wrote.
  • OAuth sign-in is more reliable: Token-exchange request bodies now percent-encode their values, so an authorization code containing a character like `+` (which some providers emit) is no longer corrupted in transit and rejected. Applies to the OpenAI Codex and Google sign-in flows; the Kimi flow (already correct) was consolidated onto the same shared, unit-tested encoder.
  • Custom hotkey bound to the "A" key no longer resets to the default on relaunch: The Dictate and Translate hotkey settings stored "no shortcut set" the same way as "bound to the A key," so relaunching the app silently reverted a shortcut like ⌥A back to the ⌥Space default. Your assigned shortcut is now preserved correctly, including A.
  • "Launch at login" toggle now reflects reality: The setting previously always showed off on launch regardless of the actual state, and a failure to enable/disable it (e.g. a system permission issue) was silently swallowed. The toggle now loads the real status on open, and any failure reverts the toggle and shows a brief inline error explaining what went wrong.
  • Paste-target activation permission prompt no longer silently denied: macOS requires an Automation usage description for Vowrite's fallback app-activation step (used when the primary activation path fails before pasting). Without it, the system auto-denied the request instead of asking you. The app now declares this permission properly.
  • Personalization page showed the wrong shortcut for scene switching: The tip under Scenes said "⌘1–⌘9"; scene-switch shortcuts actually use Control, so it now correctly reads "⌃1–⌃9" (matching the Keyboard Shortcuts page).
  • Keychain save failures and model download failures no longer silent: Saving or clearing a provider API key in Settings or during onboarding could fail (e.g. Keychain access denied) with no indication anything went wrong. The existing "Keys saved" confirmation now shows a red "Couldn't save to Keychain" variant on failure instead of quietly discarding the result. Likewise, downloading or deleting an offline Sherpa speech model now shows an inline error message on the row if it fails, instead of failing silently.

Performance

  • F-073 Polish skips reasoning on thinking-by-default models: Reasoning models (DeepSeek V4-pro, Qwen 3.5/3.6, GPT-5.5, Gemini 2.5, Kimi K2.6, Doubao thinking, GLM-5/4.7-flash/4.5-air, MiniMax M2/M2.5/M2.7, Together hybrid, SiliconFlow GLM/Qwen3, Groq qwen3-32b, Cerebras GLM, OpenRouter routed) now skip chain-of-thought during polish. Polish is a 200–500 token short task that doesn't benefit from reasoning, so this typically saves 5–30 seconds per polish call with no loss of output quality. The toggle is per-model and lives entirely in `providers.json` — no UI, no settings to fiddle with. Models that can't disable thinking (Kimi `k2-thinking-turbo`, Groq `gpt-oss-*`) now show `(thinking on, slower)` in their picker description so the trade-off is visible when you choose them.
  • Recording overlay does dramatically less work per frame: The floating recording indicator was rebuilding and reassigning its entire SwiftUI view on every 20 Hz audio-level tick, even though the view already observes those changes reactively — that manual rebuild is now gone. The History list no longer allocates a new `DateFormatter` per row on every redraw. The menu bar's microphone submenu no longer re-enumerates audio hardware on every unrelated menu bar refresh — only when the submenu is actually opened. The waveform in the overlay now drives its 60 Hz animation and its target refresh off a single timer instead of two. No visible change to how recording, processing, history, or the menu bar look or behave.
